summarization.a: The line chart shows the relationship between SF₆ plasma time and EPC (nm/cycle) at two temperatures, 300°C and 150°C.
summarization.b: The line chart illustrates the relationship between TMA pulses and EPC (nm/cycle) at two temperatures, 300°C and 150°C.

b.question: What is the main physical parameter to increase the number of TMA pulses?
b.answer: b	The main physical parameter that is changed upon increasing the number of TMA pulses which is the partial pressure of TMA. Increasing this will increase the reaction possibility.

b.question: What is the
b.answer: b	The maximum EPC is 0.31 nm/cycle.

a.question: Why is the saturated EPC higher for higher temperatures?
a.answer: It could be that the penetration depth of the radical species increases for increasing temperature.

a.question: Is there complete saturation and if not, how is this called?
a.answer: a	There is no straight line for increasing plasma time, this looks like soft-saturation.
